# API Reference (auto-generated)

**Endpoints:** 56 | **Schemas:** 105

This file is regenerated by `regenerate_mobile_app_api_client` after every codegen run.
DO NOT edit it manually — your changes will be overwritten.

## REQUIRED one-time setup: QueryClientProvider

Every generated hook uses React Query and will **crash at runtime** (`No QueryClient set, use QueryClientProvider to set one`) unless the app root is wrapped in a `QueryClientProvider`. `tsc` does NOT catch this — the app compiles fine and then white-screens on first load. Set this up ONCE in `app/_layout.tsx`:

```tsx
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from '@tanstack/react-query';

const queryClient = new QueryClient();

export default function RootLayout() {
  return (
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
      {/* ...the rest of your providers + <Stack /> / <Slot /> */}
    </QueryClientProvider>
  );
}
```

## Import paths (use these exact paths)

- **Hooks**: `import { useFoo } from '@/src/api/generated/api';`
- **Types**: `import type { FooDto } from '@/src/api/generated/schemas';`
- **Error helper**: `import { getErrorMessage, ApiError } from '@/src/api/customFetch';` — use `getErrorMessage(e)` in catch blocks (catch vars are `unknown` under strict mode).

You almost never need to import `customFetch` directly — every generated hook routes through it.

## Network layer (`src/api/customFetch.ts`)

Every hook above routes its HTTP call through `src/api/customFetch.ts` — the single network layer for your app. What it handles for you, automatically:

- Reads `BASE_URL` from `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` (with trailing slashes stripped — won't produce `host//api/foo`).
- Reads the JWT from `AsyncStorage` under the key `auth_token` (override via env var `EXPO_PUBLIC_AUTH_TOKEN_KEY`) and attaches it as `Authorization: Bearer ...` when present.
- Sets `Accept: application/json` and serializes any JSON body you pass.
- On a non-2xx response, throws `ApiError` with `.status: number` and `.body: unknown` (parsed JSON when possible, raw text otherwise). Catch it where you call the mutation/query.
- Returns the parsed JSON body directly — your hook's `data` is the response shape from the Response column below, no further unwrapping.

To save the JWT after login: `await AsyncStorage.setItem('auth_token', token)`. To log out: `await AsyncStorage.removeItem('auth_token')`. The next hook call will include or omit the header automatically.

You may edit `customFetch.ts` ONLY to change auth scheme (e.g., a different storage key, additional headers). Everything else should stay as-is.

## How to call hooks

**The `mutate()` argument shape varies per endpoint** — see the **Args** column in the tables below for the exact shape per hook. Four patterns, depending on what the endpoint takes:

- Endpoint with a **request body** → Args `{ data }` → `mutate({ data: dto })`.
- Endpoint with **path params only** → Args `{ id, ... }` → `mutate({ id })` (path params directly, NOT under `data`).
- Endpoint with **body + path params** → Args `{ data, id, ... }` → `mutate({ data: dto, id })`.
- Endpoint with **neither** → Args `void` → `mutate()` (no args).

Plain functions (used in imperative contexts — see section below) take the dto DIRECTLY (`await fooController(dto)`), with no wrapping.

```tsx
import { useAuthControllerLogin, useOnboardingControllerCompleteTask, useAttendanceControllerClockIn } from '@/src/api/generated/api';
import type { LoginDto } from '@/src/api/generated/schemas';

// Args 'void' → mutate() with no args:
const clockIn = useAttendanceControllerClockIn();
clockIn.mutate();

// Args '{ id }' (path param only) → pass path params DIRECTLY (no `data` wrapper):
const complete = useOnboardingControllerCompleteTask();
complete.mutate({ id: taskId });

// Args '{ data }' (body) → wrap body under `data`:
const { mutate: login, isPending, error } = useAuthControllerLogin();
login({ data: { email, password } as LoginDto });

// Queries (GET) — no args, or pass path/query params as the first arg object:
import { useMessagesControllerGetReceived } from '@/src/api/generated/api';
const { data, isLoading, refetch } = useMessagesControllerGetReceived();
// with query params: useMessagesControllerGetReceived({ page: 1 })
// with a path param: useGroupsControllerFindOne({ id: groupId })

// Error handling — catch vars are `unknown` under strict mode, so use
// the getErrorMessage helper instead of touching the error directly:
import { getErrorMessage, ApiError } from '@/src/api/customFetch';
try { await login({ data: { email, password } }); }
catch (e) {
  setError(getErrorMessage(e, 'Login failed'));
  // or narrow for status-specific handling:
  // if (e instanceof ApiError && e.status === 401) { /* bad credentials */ }
}
```

## Calling the API imperatively (outside components)

React Query hooks (`useFoo...`) can ONLY be called at the top level of a component or another hook — NOT inside an async callback, an auth context method, an event handler, a service module, or a `useEffect` body. In those places, call the **plain async function** instead: it is the hook name without the `use` prefix (`useAuthControllerLogin` -> `authControllerLogin`), exported from the same `@/src/api/generated/api`, takes the request DTO directly, and returns the typed response. See the "Imperative fn" column in the Hooks tables below for the exact name.

```tsx
// e.g. inside AuthContext's login callback (a hook call here would be illegal):
import { authControllerLogin } from '@/src/api/generated/api';
import { getErrorMessage } from '@/src/api/customFetch';

const login = useCallback(async (email: string, password: string) => {
  try {
    const data = await authControllerLogin({ email, password });
    await saveToken(data.token);
    setUser(data.user);
  } catch (e) {
    throw new Error(getErrorMessage(e, 'Login failed'));
  }
}, []);
```

**NEVER call `customFetch` directly** — it is the internal network layer. Use a hook (in components) or the plain function (everywhere else); both route through customFetch with the correct signature. Calling `customFetch({ url, method, data })` yourself will fail to compile — its signature is `customFetch(url, options)`, not a config object.

## Common pitfalls (the type checker WILL catch these)

- **Enum / literal-union fields** — schemas in the Schema types section shown as a quoted union like `'a' | 'b' | 'c'` are literal unions, not `string`. To assign such a field, use the generated const (`FooEnum.someValue`) or annotate your state (`useState<FooEnum>('someValue')`). A plain `string` will not type-check, and **never cast with `as any`** — that defeats every benefit of codegen.
- **`data` from a query hook is `T | undefined`** (undefined while loading or on error). Guard at the top of the component: `if (isLoading || !data) return <Loading />;` — TypeScript then narrows `data` to the typed response and you can access fields safely.
- **Don't guess response field names** — every schema's field signature is listed inline in the **Schema types** section below. Read it once and use the exact fields shown.
- **Mutation hooks expose `isPending`, NOT `isLoading`** (React Query v5). `const { mutate, isPending } = useFooMutation()`. Writing `isLoading` returns `undefined` and TypeScript rejects it. Query hooks (GET) still use `isLoading`.
- **Conditional queries** — never wrap a hook call in `if` (rules of hooks). To gate a query on a dependency, pass `enabled` in the options: `useFooHook(params, { query: { enabled: !!userId } })`. The hook still runs every render, but the request is skipped until `enabled` is true.
- **Query call shape ≠ mutation call shape.** For GET hooks, path params are POSITIONAL (not packed into an object): `useGroupsControllerFindOne(id, { query: { enabled: !!id } })` — NOT `useGroupsControllerFindOne({ id }, ...)`. When both path and query params exist, the shape is `(pathParam, { queryParams }, options)`: e.g., `useExpensesControllerFindByGroup(groupId, { page, limit })`. For mutations (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE), `mutate(...)` takes a single object combining body and path params: `mutate({ data: { ... }, id })`. The Args column in the Hooks table below shows the exact call shape per hook — read it.
- **All query-param object fields are optional unless their entry omits the `?`.** If the Schema types section shows `FooParams = { page?: number; limit?: number }`, you can call `useFooHook({ limit: 5 })` and omit `page` — orval emits `?` on every optional swagger param. Do not defensively pass `page: 1` just because tsc shouted about unrelated errors elsewhere; the type accepts what it says it accepts.

## Schema types

Import from `@/src/api/generated/schemas`. Field signatures (derived from swagger.json) below:

- `AcademicYearListItemDto`: `{ id: string; name: string; startDate: string; endDate: string; isActive: boolean; createdAt: string }`
- `AcademicYearResponseDto`: `{ id: string; name: string; startDate: string; endDate: string; isActive: boolean }`
- `ActiveAcademicYearDto`: `{ id: string; name: string }`
- `AddClassHistoryDto`: `{ classId: string; academicYearId: string; action: string }`
- `AddEnrollmentDto`: `{ classId: string }`
- `AddParticipantDto`: `{ studentId?: string; teacherId?: string; groupId?: string }`
- `AddPhotoDto`: `{ fileId: string; caption?: string }`
- `AlbumDetailDto`: `{ id: string; title: string; description: string | null; className: string | null; eventName: string | null; photos: GalleryPhotoDto[] }`
- `AlbumListItemDto`: `{ id: string; title: string; description: string | null; className: string | null; eventName: string | null; photoCount: number; coverUrl: string | null; createdAt: string }`
- `AlertSettingDto`: `{ teacherId: string; teacherName: string; delayMinutes: number; enabled: boolean; channel: string }`
- `AssignTeacherDto`: `{ teacherId: string }`
- `AttendanceControllerGetOverviewParams`: `unknown`
- `AttendanceOverviewResponseDto`: `{ sessions: SessionSummaryDto[]; classSummary: ClassSummaryDto; studentBreakdown: StudentBreakdownDto[] }`
- `AttendanceSummaryDto`: `{ totalSessions: number; present: number; absent: number; late: number; excused: number; percentage: number; perClass: PerClassAttendanceDto[] }`
- `AwardIssuanceDto`: `{ id: string; awardId: string; awardName: string; awardIcon: string | null; note: string | null; issuedAt: string; recipientName: string; recipientKind: string; studentId: string | null; teacherId: string | null; photoUrl: string | null }`
- `AwardListItemDto`: `{ id: string; name: string; description: string | null; icon: string | null; issuedCount: number }`
- `AwardsControllerFindIssuancesParams`: `unknown`
- `BulkAttendanceDto`: `{ sessionId: string; studentAttendance: StudentAttendanceEntry[]; teacherAttendance?: TeacherAttendanceEntry[] }`
- `BulkAttendanceResponseDto`: `{ success: boolean; savedCount: number }`
- `CheckAlertsResultDto`: `{ created: number; checkedSessions: number }`
- `ClassDetailResponseDto`: `{ id: string; name: string; grade: string; description: string | null; teachers: ClassTeacherDto[]; students: ClassStudentDto[]; sessions: ClassSessionInfoDto[]; metrics: ClassMetricInfoDto[] }`
- `ClassHistoryInfoDto`: `{ id: string; classId: string; className: string; academicYearName: string; action: string; date: string }`
- `ClassHistoryResponseDto`: `{ id: string; studentId: string; classId: string; className: string; academicYearId: string; academicYearName: string; action: string; date: string }`
- `ClassListItemDto`: `{ id: string; name: string; grade: string; description: string | null; studentCount: number; teachers: ClassTeacherDto[]; nextSessionDate: string | null }`
- `ClassMetricInfoDto`: `{ id: string; name: string; type: string; description: string | null }`
- `ClassSessionInfoDto`: `{ id: string; date: string; attendanceSubmitted: boolean; termName: string | null }`
- `ClassStudentDto`: `{ id: string; name: string; photoFileId: string | null; photoUrl: string | null; enrollmentStatus: string }`
- `ClassSummaryDto`: `{ totalSessions: number; averageAttendance: number }`
